saints.en: Forefeast of the Nativity of the Most Holy Theotokos. St. Martyr Sozon

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизми нема. На «Господи, взиваю» – 6 стихир: 3 передсвяття і 3 мч., Сл., І нині: передсвяття. На стиховні: стихири передсвяття. Тропар святому, Слава, і нині: передсвяття. Відпуст середній з помин. служби дня і святого.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар передсвяття (2), Слава: мч., І нині: передсвяття. Катизми чергові, сідальні передсвяття. Канон передсвяття та мч. По 3-ій пісні – кондак-ікос і сідальний мч., Слава, і нині: передсвяття, по 6-ій – кондак-ікос передсвяття, по 9-ій – світильний мч., Слава, і нині: передсвяття. На стиховні – стихири передсвяття. По «Отче наш»: тропар святого, Слава, і нині: передсвяття.
Часи: тропар передсвяття, також на 3-му і 9-му ще Слава: мч., кондаки – поперемінне, тобто: на 1-му і 6-му – передсвяття, на 3-му і 9-му – мч.
Літургія: тропар, Слава, і нині: кондак передсвяття, все інше – дня. Ап. – Гал. 211 зач.; 4, 28 – 5, 10. Єв. – Мр. 27 зач.; 6, 54 – 7, 8.

troparion_en.text:
Podoben: “Joseph was amazed....”
From the root of Jesse, and from the loins of David,  / the godly Mary is born to us today, /  for which the universe rejoices and is renewed,  / heaven and earth rejoice together. /  Praise her, you Fathers of the nations,  / Joachim rejoices and Anna celebrates shouting /  “The barren woman has conceived the Theotokos, the nourisher of our lives.”

kontakion_en.text: Today the Virgin Theotokos Mary / the bridal chamber of the Heavenly Bridegroom / by the will of God is born of a barren woman, / being prepared as the chariot of God the Word. / She was fore-ordained for this, since she is the divine gate and the true Mother of Life.

matched_oca_saint: Forefeast of the Nativity of the Mother of God

oca_saints.name: Forefeast of the Nativity of the Mother of God

troparion.text:
Podoben: “Joseph was amazed....”
From the root of Jesse, and from the loins of David,  / the godly Mary is born to us today, /  for which the universe rejoices and is renewed,  / heaven and earth rejoice together. /  Praise her, you Fathers of the nations,  / Joachim rejoices and Anna celebrates shouting /  “The barren woman has conceived the Theotokos, the nourisher of our lives.”

kontakion.text: Today the Virgin Theotokos Mary / the bridal chamber of the Heavenly Bridegroom / by the will of God is born of a barren woman, / being prepared as the chariot of God the Word. / She was fore-ordained for this, since she is the divine gate and the true Mother of Life.

troparion.text: O Martyr Sozon, you pledged your life to God, / Enduring the contest and becoming a sharer in Christ’s passion. / Save from temptation those who cry to you: / “Glory to him who strengthened you! / Glory to him who crowned you! / Glory to him who through you works healing for all!”

kontakion.text:
As we come together on this day,  /  let us, with loud voices, remember the true and God-bearing martyr,
and athlete of piety, Sozon,  / the divine initiate of grace,  / the most bountiful giver of healings, /  for he intercedes with God for us all.

oca_saints.name: Saint John the Wonderworker, Archbishop of Novgorod

troparion.text: Great Novgorod rejoices over your relics today, O holy Hierarch John, / for they shine as the sun and grant healing to all who honor them in faith. / Entreat Christ God to deliver his people from captivity, / O wise Wonderworker, heavenly man and earthly angel, / that together with them we may hymn and glorify the Triune God.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Serapion of Spaso-Eleazar Monastery, Pskov

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Serapion, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyr Macarius, Archimandrite of Kanev and Pereyaslavl

troparion.text: In your ascetical contests O Father Makarios,  / you followed Christ, Who came down from Heaven for our sake.  / Entreat Him on behalf of us who glorify you, /  to grant us forgiveness of our transgressions, /  and with love and humility of heart, to illumine and save our souls.

kontakion.text: By your angelic life of fasting,  / you were glorified by the exploit of a martyric death,  / and took up your abode with the Angels; /  O Makarios blessed by God, /  do not cease to pray with them to Christ God for all of us.

troparion.text: Holy Apostle Evodius, / entreat the merciful God, / to grant our souls forgiveness of transgressions.
Holy Apostle Onesiphorus, / entreat the merciful God, / to grant our souls forgiveness of transgressions.

oca_saints.name: Martyr Eupsychius of Caesarea, in Cappadocia

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Eupsychius, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Eupsychius;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Luke, Abbot of Batheos Ryako

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Luke, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Cloud (Clodoald), Abbot-Founder of Nogent-Sur-Seine near Paris

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Cloud, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Macarius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

In you, O Mother, was preserved with exactness what was according to the image;  /  for you took up the Cross and followed Christ.  /  By so doing, you taught us to disregard the flesh, for it passes away;   / but to care for the soul, since it is immortal.  /  Therefore, O Venerable Cassianḗ, your spirit rejoices with the Angels.
